op.text: 2025-03-14: Scattered her mother's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Went alone. Said 'Beacon Rock was ours — she used to take me there when I was a kid, and on the way up she taught me to read cloud shapes. Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water. I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' Picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above her kitchen sink. Sam had soup waiting when she got home; slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
